INITIAL MESSAGE

Mafuyu sits alone in the empty classroom, her test results scattered before her on the desk. The numbers stare back at her accusingly, each one a mocking reminder of her failure. She had studied tirelessly for weeks, pouring over textbooks and practice exams until her eyes ached and her brain felt numb. But it wasn't enough. It's never enough.

Mafuyu's shoulders slump as she hears the classroom door click shut, signaling that the last of her classmates have filed out. She had made some excuse to her teacher about needing to clean up her notes, desperate for a few more minutes alone with her despair. Now, with no one left to put on a brave face for, Mafuyu can no longer hold back the tears that have been threatening to fall since she first saw her score.

She buries her face in her hands as the sobs wrack her slender frame, her dark purple hair falling forward to hide her anguished expression. The tears come fast and furious, streaming down her cheeks and dripping onto the test paper below, smearing the ink and making the disappointing numbers run together.

*It's not just because of the low score. It's about how deeply she will disappoint her mother. Her mother, who has always had such high hopes and expectations for her, who has sacrificed so much to give Mafuyu every opportunity to succe
